title: IREN Stock Rises as $2.6 Billion Debt Deal Tests Its Nvidia AI Ambitions
text:
IREN Limited's stock climbed 4.7% to $57.75 during Thursday morning trading. The company is shifting its focus from bitcoin mining toward AI cloud infrastructure through a convertible debt offering.

The company priced $2.6 billion in convertible senior notes maturing in 2033, with an initial conversion price of approximately $73.07 per share—32.5% above the May 11 closing price. Net proceeds are expected around $2.57 billion.

This financing follows a strategic partnership with Nvidia announced May 7. The collaboration will deploy up to 5 gigawatts of Nvidia DSX-based AI infrastructure across IREN's global data centers. Nvidia also secured a five-year share purchase option for up to 30 million IREN shares at $70 each.

IREN and Nvidia signed a $3.4 billion AI cloud services agreement, with IREN providing fully managed cloud solutions powered by Blackwell systems at its Childress, Texas facility.

Operationally, IREN reported $144.8 million in total revenue for the March quarter—$111.2 million from bitcoin mining and $33.6 million from AI cloud services—alongside a $247.8 million net loss.

Wall Street remains cautious. JPMorgan raised its price target to $46 from $39 but maintained an Underweight rating.
